name: Enuma Elish
shortDescription: The Enuma Elish is the Babylonian creation myth, describing the origin of the cosmos, the gods, and humanity.
description: The Enuma Elish, meaning "When on High," is the Babylonian creation epic. It narrates the primordial state of the universe as a watery chaos, personified by Apsu (fresh water) and Tiamat (salt water). Their offspring, the younger gods, disturb the peace of Apsu, leading him to plot their destruction. Ea, one of the younger gods, discovers Apsu's plan and kills him. Enraged, Tiamat wages war against the gods. Marduk, a hero god, volunteers to fight Tiamat, but only if he is recognized as king of the gods. The other gods agree, and Marduk defeats Tiamat, splitting her body in two to create the sky and the earth. He then organizes the cosmos, establishing the stars, planets, and calendar. Finally, Marduk creates humanity from the blood of Kingu, Tiamat's consort, to serve the gods. The Enuma Elish served not only as a cosmological explanation but also as a justification for Marduk's supremacy and the central importance of Babylon. It was recited during the Akitu festival, a new year celebration, to reaffirm the cosmic order and the power of the king. The epic highlights themes of divine conflict, the establishment of order from chaos, and the role of humanity in the divine plan.

sources.title: The Ancient Near East: An Anthology of Texts and Pictures
sources.author: James B. Pritchard

sources.title: Myths from Mesopotamia: Creation, The Flood, Gilgamesh and Others

sources.title: Before Philosophy: The Intellectual Adventure of Ancient Man
